name: Larry Young
abstract: Larry Young (also known as Khalid Yasin [Abdul Aziz]; October 7, 1940 – March 30, 1978) was an American jazz organist and occasional pianist. Young's early work was strongly influenced by the soul jazz of Jimmy Smith, but he later pioneered a more experimental, modal approach to the Hammond B-3.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz on the Hammond

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: There is Herbie, McLaughlin, Chick, Billy Hart, [Jack] DeJohnette, Larry Young, Badal [Roy], Collin Walcott, Don Alias, and a guy named Harold Williams... I knew Jack. A couple – “Hi. How you doing?” [?] said, “Yeah, how you doing?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I was always fascinated with Tony Williams’ Lifetime band. That band was light-years ahead of a lot of organ trios, with Larry Young and John McLaughlin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.evidence: And through him I heard Larry Young and then that led to a whole path of organ players
